project.title: Proteomic response of macrophages to human cancer Raji cells
project.projectDescription: In order to determine changes in phosphorylation events in macrophages upon phagocytosis of cancer cells, mouse macrophages were co-cultured with human Raji cancer cells. Macrophages were collected as 0, 1, 15, 60 and 120 minutes after incubation. Phosphosite abundances were compared between timepoints to determine differential phospho signaling over time.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Raji cells were washed and macrophages isolated after co-culture. Cells were lysed and centrifuged to isolate proteins from macrophages. Proteins were reduced and alkylated, then digested into peptides using trypsin in overnight incubation. Samples were then incubated with TMT reagents for labeling and then pooled. The pooled, TMT-labeled, peptide mixture was phosphoenriched first by high-select TiO2 phosphopeptide enrichment, and then high select Fe-NTA phosphoenrichment. Enriched pools were then separated via Ultimate 3000 RSLC nano and injected into the Thermo Lumos tribrid mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw spectra were searched against a mouse SwissProt database using Byonic search engine run through Proteome Discoverer. Phosphorylation (STY) was set as a variable modification along with oxidation (M) and deamidation (N,Q). Methylthio (C) and TMT6plex (N-term, K) were set as fixed modifications. PSMs were filtered for FDR <1%.

references.referenceLine: Wang B, Cao X, Garcia-Mansfield K, Zhou J, Manousopoulou A, Pirrotte P, Wang Y, Wang LD, Feng M. Phosphoproteomic Profiling Reveals mTOR Signaling in Sustaining Macrophage Phagocytosis of Cancer Cells. Cancers (Basel). 2024 16(24):4238

Characteristics[ReductionReagent]: Reducing Reagent (Sigma-Aldrich, St. Louis, MI, USA; #4381664)
Characteristics[Label]: TMT-11plex reagents (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA; #A37725)
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: High Select TiO2 phosphopeptide enrichment kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA; #A32993)
High Select Fe-NTA phosphopeptide enrichment kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA; #A32992)
The TiO2- and Fe-NTA-enriched phosphopeptide fractions were acquired separately.
Comment[Instrument]: U3000 RSLCnano liquid chromatography system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA)
Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Tribrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific)
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: precursor mass tolerance was set to 10 ppm
